page: Amazon Web Services c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture utilizing an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core results placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, cryptographic execution, and database operations fall within the average range. However, memory performance presents a notable tradeoff; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%. Similarly, while LLM inference with small 135M models is average, prompt processing for medium 7B models is weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6a.2xlarge offers a dedicated AMD EPYC compute profile suitable for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory throughput or local storage. It is well-suited for tasks such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core performance and cryptographic operations remain average. A key hardware tradeoff is its memory bandwidth, as large-block uncached reads and writes perform in the bottom 25%. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for compute-heavy workloads that do not depend on high memory throughput. It is suited for software compilation, static web serving, and general compute tasks.
